customer_goal: make Gaussian rendering faster when vectorized

verdict.summary: The emitted-prefix global radix path reverses the original result: the custom renderer is faster than pinned gsplat at B4 and B8 on both real scans, while preserving strict output and fidelity acceptance.
verdict.what_is_supported: For the frozen L4 protocol, the final custom B8 path sustains 39.40 images/s on Home Scan and 74.76 images/s on Garage, respectively 1.55x and 1.90x the pinned gsplat GPU throughput.
verdict.what_is_not_supported: The evidence does not support a claim of many-times-higher throughput than gsplat at vectorized batch sizes, near-linear scaling with batch size, replacement of RTX or OVRTX, or an L40 result.
verdict.vectorization_interpretation: The goal is met as higher absolute throughput for vectorized requests. The custom renderer itself is nearly throughput-flat from B1 through B8 rather than becoming proportionally faster as batch size grows.
verdict.remaining_hardware_gate: Repeat the frozen matrix and boundary search on an L40 before closing the L40-specific acceptance issue.

protocol.coverage: Every source view appears exactly once at every reported batch size.
protocol.camera_match: Custom input and gsplat replay use the persisted exact view matrices, intrinsics, scene IDs, and source-view population.
protocol.custom_path: Headless Isaac Sim MinimalRendering, USD/Fabric CUDA camera ingestion, CustomCudaBackend, output-contract validation, and artifact bookkeeping.
protocol.gsplat_path: Direct gsplat contract-exact replay of the persisted custom trajectory.
protocol.warmup: Shader, JIT, extension, adaptive-capacity, and per-renderer warmup occur outside acceptance timing.
protocol.comparison_provenance: The B1/B4/B8 gsplat values are pinned from the corrected issue-9 record on the same replacement L4, driver, dependencies, scenes, and camera contracts; B12 was rerun directly for this investigation.
protocol.timing_scope_warning: GPU-event throughput is the primary renderer comparison. Custom integrated wall time includes Isaac/Fabric and validation work that direct gsplat wall time does not.

profiling.scope: Nsight Systems full-process traces of the exact Home trajectory. Warmup and adaptive retry work remain in the per-useful-view diagnostic numerator.
profiling.mechanism: The nondeterministic path globally radix-sorts only the emitted intersection prefix and marks tile ranges linearly. The deterministic path retains device-only segmented ordering.
profiling.synchronization_tradeoff: Each new or invalidated projection copies the emitted count to the host and synchronizes the current CUDA stream before global radix sort. A validated projection-cache hit reuses sorted intersections without that synchronization.
profiling.diagnosis: The original segmented ordering and grouping bottleneck is removed. Global sorting remains the largest single B8 kernel stage, closely followed by projection and rasterization.

cases.gsplat_failure: CUDA OOM during warmup: a 2.56 GiB allocation was requested with 20.10 GiB already in use and 1.93 GiB free.

cases.failure_stage: mandatory output-contract depth validation after the renderer completed
cases.failure: CUDA OOM requesting 108.00 MiB with 103.38 MiB free; the process used 21.89 GiB of the 22.03 GiB L4 and PyTorch had allocated 20.95 GiB.
cases.boundary_interpretation: B30 is the largest passing exact divisor tested; B40 is the next exact divisor and fails the full acceptance path at physical device memory, not at the backend workspace policy.

high_batch_stress.non_monotonic_capacity_note: Workspace capacity is not monotonic in batch size because view-population packing changes which camera phases share a batch and adaptive growth responds to the first and later demand peaks.

bugs_found_and_fixed.bug: Per-tile segmented radix ordering and grouping dominated B8 kernel time.
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: Sort the emitted intersection prefix globally and derive tile ranges in one linear pass.
bugs_found_and_fixed.evidence: Home B8 diagnostic kernel time fell from 72.77 to 28.53 ms per useful view.

bugs_found_and_fixed.bug: Adaptive growth retained the old full workspace while allocating its replacement, causing a transient double-workspace OOM at Home B12.
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: After the synchronized overflow read, release old workspace storage first and restore the prior plan if replacement allocation fails.
bugs_found_and_fixed.evidence: Home B12, B16, and B20 pass under the 20 GiB workspace cap; injected allocator-failure restoration is unit tested.

bugs_found_and_fixed.bug: The Kit extension independently forced a 2.0 growth factor after the backend default was reduced, so the production Isaac path still doubled large workspaces.
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: The extension now inherits the backend default unless a caller explicitly overrides it.
bugs_found_and_fixed.evidence: The unchanged Home B24 case moved from a rejected 26.13 GB plan to a passing 16.68 GB workspace.

bugs_found_and_fixed.bug: A hard workspace ceiling rejected a headroom target even when exact counter-observed demand fit.
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: Relax spare headroom to exact observed demand before failing closed, and record the adjustment in telemetry.
bugs_found_and_fixed.evidence: Home B40 advanced past renderer allocation and rendering; its remaining failure is physical L4 memory during mandatory validation.

bugs_found_and_fixed.bug: The prior result record labeled six GPU-derived gsplat rates as synchronized wall throughput.
bugs_found_and_fixed.fix: Recompute those fields and ratios from the preserved synchronized wall means.
bugs_found_and_fixed.evidence: Corrected in ff26c0f; the numerical change is below 0.15% and does not change a verdict.

correctness.compute_sanitizer: ERROR SUMMARY: 0 errors on final tested code commit 33591d5
correctness.verdict: Performance, fidelity, Isaac integration, capacity correctness, and sanitizer correctness all pass within the reported L4 scope.

limitations: This is L4 evidence, not the requested L40 result.
Only two real scanned scenes, two modest resolutions, and one frozen walkthrough family are covered.
The comparison is against the same screen-space EWA equation family in gsplat, not against OVRTX or the proprietary RTX renderer.
The custom path is a Gaussian renderer used alongside Isaac Sim; it does not replace RTX rendering of meshes, lighting, shadows, reflections, or other scene content.
No combined RTX-plus-custom compositor frame-time, dynamic-robot scene, sensor-stack, 720p/1080p, multi-scene, or long-duration stability result is claimed here.
Moving projection-cache misses intentionally incur an emitted-count host read and CUDA-stream synchronization before global sort.
Memory remains O(batch times emitted intersections); the full Home B40 acceptance path exhausts a 22.03 GiB L4.
Nsight stage totals include warmup and retry work and are diagnostic rather than acceptance timing.

evidence.availability: Raw artifacts are intentionally ignored by Git; checksums bind this committed record to the preserved local evidence.
